description: This stacked bar chart displays the number percentage distribution of inclusions across three Fe-15Mn-xAl-C low-density steel samples (1#, 2#, and 3#) with increasing aluminum content (approximately 0.87 wt%, 2.77 wt%, and 4.70 wt%, respectively). The y-axis represents number percentage (%) ranging from 0 to 100, while the x-axis distinguishes the three experimental samples. The legend identifies eight inclusion categories: single-phase Al₂O₃, AlN, MnS, TiN, and composite phases Al₂O₃-MnS, AlN-MnS, TiN-MnS, and Al₂O₃-AlN-MnS. The chart reveals that as Al content increases, the number percentage of Al₂O₃ decreases significantly while AlN increases progressively; single MnS inclusions peak at intermediate Al content then decline; TiN remains relatively constant; Al₂O₃-MnS composites increase whereas AlN-MnS and TiN-MnS decrease; and Al₂O₃-AlN-MnS ternary composites appear in samples 1# and 2# but are absent in 3#. These quantitative trends support the thermodynamic analysis that increasing Al content alters solidification routes and delays the precipitation of nitride and sulfide inclusions.
